title: Christ delivered me when bound
publicDomainBasis: William Cowper (1731–1800), John Bacchus Dykes (1823–1876). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Christ delivered me when bound,
And when bleeding, healed my wound;
Sought me wand’ring, set me right,
Turned my darkness into light.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Can a woman’s tender care
Cease towards the child she bare?
Yes, she may forgetful be;
Yet will He remember me.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
His is an unchanging love,
Higher than the heights above;
Deeper than the depths beneath,
Free and faithful, strong as death.

sections.label: Verse 4
Verse 5
sections.text:
Lord, it is my chief complaint
That my love is weak and faint;
Yet I love Thee, and adore:
Oh for grace to love Thee more.
